app.name: Beaverr
app.description: Beaverr walks you through your household finances step by step — income, fixed costs, and what remains for everyday spending. Plain language, one clear monthly picture.

sidebar.lockedTabMessage: Submit the full questionnaire to unlock Expenses, Budget, Goals, Savings, Summary, and Reminders.

questionnaireBanner.title: Questionnaire completion
questionnaireBanner.progressHint: Finish the full questionnaire to unlock Expenses, Budget, Goals, Savings, Summary, and Reminders.
questionnaireBanner.estimateWarning: Figures are estimates until you complete the full questionnaire.

welcome.title: Welcome to Beaverr
welcome.body: Create an account to save your household budget securely and access it on any device.
welcome.goToLogin: Already have an account? Sign in

completeProfile.title: Complete your account
completeProfile.helper: One last step — choose a username and preferences to finish setting up your account.
completeProfile.switchGoogleA11y: Remove this account and sign in with a different Google account
completeProfile.switchOAuthLink: Sign in with a different account
completeProfile.switchOAuthA11y: Remove this account and sign in again with a different account
completeProfile.deleteIncompleteA11y: Delete this account and return to the welcome screen
completeProfile.deleteIncompleteConfirmMessage: You haven't finished setting up yet. This permanently removes this Beaverr account and returns you to the welcome screen.

login.title: Sign in
login.helper: Use the email and password for your Beaverr account.

signup.title: Create Account
signup.goToLogin: Already have an account? Sign in
signup.consentLabel: I agree to GDPR-compliant data processing.

errors.required: Please fill in all required fields.
errors.consentRequired: Please accept the privacy terms to continue.
errors.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters.
errors.passwordWeak: Password must be at least 8 characters and include a letter and a number.
errors.usernameInvalid: Use 3–20 characters: letters, numbers, and underscores only.
errors.usernameCheckFailed: Could not verify this username. Please try again.
errors.signInFailed: Sign-in failed. Check your email and password.
errors.socialSignInFailed: Social sign-in failed. Please try again.
errors.signUpFailed: Could not create account. Please try again.
errors.emailAlreadyRegistered: An account with this email already exists. Sign in instead.
errors.confirmEmail: Check your email to confirm your account, then sign in.
errors.oauthLocalhostMobile: Google sign-in cannot use localhost on a phone. On your computer run Expo with LAN (npx expo start --lan), open the app at http://YOUR-PC-IP:8081 on this phone, and add that callback URL in Supabase → Authentication → Redirect URLs.
errors.oauthRedirectOriginPlaceholder: EXPO_PUBLIC_AUTH_REDIRECT_ORIGIN is still a placeholder (e.g. 192.168.x.x). Set your PC's real LAN IP in .env.local, restart Expo, and add http://YOUR-IP:8081/auth/callback in Supabase Redirect URLs.
errors.oauthPkceMismatch: Sign-in callback did not match where you started (e.g. localhost vs LAN IP). Open the app at one address only, remove EXPO_PUBLIC_AUTH_REDIRECT_ORIGIN on laptop, and try Google sign-in again.
errors.accountExists: An account with this email already exists. Sign in with email and password instead.
errors.misconfigured: Account services are temporarily unavailable. Please try again later.

notice.confirmEmail: Account created. Check your email to confirm, then sign in here.

auth.signOutHelper: Sign out on this device. Your data stays saved in your account.

datePicker.hint: Type a date or pick a suggestion

welcome.heading: Let's set up your budget
welcome.description: For accurate insights and monthly stats, we'll ask a few questions about your income, costs, and everyday spending — it won't take long. Prefer to move fast? Choose quick setup and fill in the rest whenever you're ready.
welcome.illustrationA11y: Illustration of someone planning their time with a clock and hourglass
welcome.title: Beaver
welcome.subtitle: Not rich. Just ready.

consent.title: Your privacy
consent.bodyIntro: Beaverr stores your household budget in your account on secure, EU-hosted cloud infrastructure. When you sign in, your data syncs so you can continue on another device.
consent.bodyRights: We do not sell your data or use it for third-party advertising. You can sign out, withdraw consent, and delete your data from Account settings at any time.
consent.bodyLegal: We process your personal data in compliance with the GDPR (EU General Data Protection Regulation) to provide the budgeting service you signed up for.
consent.body: Beaverr stores your household budget in your account on secure, EU-hosted cloud infrastructure. When you sign in, your data syncs so you can continue on another device. We do not sell your data or use it for third-party advertising. You can sign out, withdraw consent, and delete your data from Account settings at any time. We process your personal data in compliance with the GDPR (EU General Data Protection Regulation) to provide the budgeting service you signed up for.
consent.checkbox: I agree to GDPR-compliant data processing and want to continue
consent.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a person holding a GDPR privacy shield and flag

setupMode.title: How would you like to set up Beaverr?
setupMode.body: The more detail you add, the more accurate your plan. Full setup gives you the clearest picture of your household finances.
setupMode.illustrationA11y: Illustration of an onboarding checklist on a screen

full.title: Full questionnaire
full.helper: Walk through every section — income, housing, transport, health, and more — for the most accurate budget.

quick.title: Quick setup
quick.helper: Enter essentials only — name, household, employment, income, rent, and utilities — then explore the dashboard.

exit.discardMessageFirstTime: Your saved progress and answers will be deleted. You will return to the dashboard.
exit.discardMessageQuickSetup: Your saved progress and answers will be deleted. You will return to the dashboard.
exit.discardMessageReturning: Your in-progress changes will be discarded and your previous questionnaire will be restored.

leave.title: Leave questionnaire?
leave.message: You will return to the dashboard. Your progress is saved and you can continue later.

name.title: What should we call you?
name.helper: We use this to personalise your plan and milestone messages.
name.label: Your name

rent.helper: Your typical monthly rent — an estimate is fine for now.

income.title: Your monthly income
income.helper: Net amount — what actually lands in your account each month.
income.helperNotWorking: If you receive benefits, support, or other regular income, add it here. Otherwise you can skip.

utilities.helper: Combined monthly utilities (gas, electricity, water). Estimate is OK.

splashHousehold.heading: Tell us more about your family
splashHousehold.body: Tell us who lives in your household — just you, you and a partner, or children too. We'll ask for your partner's display name and each child's age so the right income and cost questions appear in later sections.
splashHousehold.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a family standing together

type.title: Who's at home
type.helper: This helps us set up income and cost fields for your whole household.
type.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a person relaxing on a sofa at home

partnerName.title: What should we call your partner?
partnerName.helper: Used only to personalise labels like 'Sarah's income'. Nothing else.
partnerName.label: Partner's display name
partnerName.validation: Please enter your partner's display name

children.title: Do you have any children?
children.helper: This unlocks a dedicated section for children's costs.
children.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a family with children

numChildren.title: How many children do you have?
numChildren.helper: Each child gets their own cost section later in the setup.

childDetails.title: Child {{n}} — a couple of quick details
childDetails.helper: The name is just for display. The age group shows the right cost fields.
childDetails.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a parent and child flying a kite

s2.heading: Where you live and what you do
s2.body: We'll ask for your country, city, and currency, plus citizenship and employment for each adult. If anyone needs a visa or residence permit, we'll capture permit type and renewal costs so they land in your plan.
s2.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a person with a globe

splashResidence.heading: Citizenship and employment
splashResidence.body: We'll confirm citizenship for each adult and child, then capture visa or residence permit details where needed. Employment comes next so your plan reflects who works and how.
splashResidence.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a person holding an identity card

location.title: Where do you live?
location.helper: We use this to pre-fill local costs and show relevant financial context.
location.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a person checking their location on a phone
location.countryPreAlphaNote: Only Czech Republic is supported at the moment. Other countries will be supported soon.

citizenship.title: Are you a citizen of the Czech Republic?
citizenship.helper: This helps us show the right employment and residency context for your household.
citizenship.allHelper: This helps us show the right employment and residency context for your household.
citizenship.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a person holding an ID card
citizenship.partnerTitle: Is {{name}} a citizen of the Czech Republic?
citizenship.partnerHelper: We ask because visa and permit costs may apply separately for each adult.
citizenship.childTitle: Is {{name}} a citizen of the Czech Republic?
citizenship.childTitleNumber: Is child {{n}} a citizen of the Czech Republic?
citizenship.childHelper: Children of non-citizen parents may need their own permit — we use this for renewal planning.
citizenship.validation: Please answer whether you are a Czech citizen

residencePermit.title: What is your residence permit or visa?
residencePermit.helper: We use this to estimate renewal timing and cost so you can plan ahead.
residencePermit.partnerTitle: What is {{name}}'s residence permit or visa?
residencePermit.partnerHelper: We use this to estimate renewal timing and cost for your partner.
residencePermit.childTitle: What is {{name}}'s residence permit or visa?
residencePermit.childTitleNumber: What is child {{n}}'s residence permit or visa?
residencePermit.childHelper: We use this to estimate renewal timing and cost for your child.
residencePermit.endDateHelper: When your current permit expires — we'll remind you before renewal.
residencePermit.renewalCostHelper: Typical fee for renewing this permit type.
residencePermit.validationStartDate: Please enter when your permit started
residencePermit.validationEndDate: Please enter when your permit expires
residencePermit.validationRenewalCost: Please enter the estimated renewal cost

occupation.title: Tell us about your work
occupation.helper: This affects your income section and some cost fields — for example, health insurance.
occupation.partnerHelper: Affects income fields and insurance context.

s3.heading: Now Your Income and Savings
s3.body: In this section you'll enter take-home pay for you and your partner, any other regular income, and what you already have in savings. We'll also note savings goals so your flexible budget leaves room for them.
s3.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a person with a wallet and payment card

splash.heading: Set your budgeting strategies
splash.body: We'll ask what you want from budgeting — clearer spending, spending less, or building savings — and any specific targets you're working toward. Your answers shape how we present your flexible budget and daily spending allowance.
splash.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a person planning a budget with charts

goalIntents.title: What do you want Beaverr to help with?
goalIntents.helper: Select all that apply. (You may select more than one option)
goalIntents.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a person setting personal financial goals
goalIntents.intentSpendLessDesc: Cut bills, subscriptions, debt, and waste
goalIntents.intentBuildMoreDesc: Save, invest, or hit a financial target
goalIntents.validationType: Please select at least one option

goalMode.title: How do you want to save?
goalMode.helper: Choose whether you are saving toward a specific target or an ongoing monthly amount.
goalMode.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a person saving coins into a piggy bank
goalMode.targetDesc: e.g. 250,000 Kč for a car by a target date
goalMode.ongoingDesc: Set aside a fixed amount each month with no end date
goalMode.validation: Please choose how you want to save

goalDetails.targetHelper: Enter what you are saving for, how much you need, and when you want to reach it.
goalDetails.ongoingHelper: How much do you want to set aside each month?
goalDetails.validationOngoingAmount: Please enter your monthly savings target

yourIncome.helperSelfEmployed: After business expenses and tax — your take-home average.
yourIncome.helperStudent: Your regular stipend or scholarship amount.
yourIncome.helperNotWorking: We'll skip income — you can add other sources below if you have any.
yourIncome.helperOther: Your best estimate of average monthly take-home.
yourIncome.notWorkingNote: No income to enter — you can add other sources below.
yourIncome.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a person receiving money on their phone

otherIncome.title: Any extra sources of income?
otherIncome.helper: Rental income, dividends, financial support — anything regular.
otherIncome.selectHelper: Browse categories and tap the income sources you receive. You will enter amounts on the next step.
otherIncome.fillHelper: Enter the amount and how often you receive each source you selected.
otherIncome.noSearchResults: No income sources match your search.
otherIncome.selectPrompt: Tap the income sources that apply to you.
otherIncome.helperRequired: Neither you nor your partner has employment income recorded. Add at least one regular income source so we can build your budget.
otherIncome.helperRequiredSolo: You don't have employment income recorded. Add at least one regular income source so we can build your budget.
otherIncome.requiredNote: At least one income source is required to continue.
otherIncome.validationNoIncome: We can't build your budget without income. Add at least one regular source — such as benefits, rental income, or financial support — or go back and enter {{name}}'s salary or your own income.
otherIncome.validationNoIncomeSolo: We can't build your budget without income. Add at least one regular source — such as benefits, rental income, or financial support.
otherIncome.validationOtherAmount: Please enter the amount for each income source.
otherIncome.validationOtherLabel: Please enter a name for each custom income source.

savings.title: Do you have savings?
savings.helper: Your balance shows how many months of expenses you have covered.
savings.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a person saving coins into a piggy bank

s4.heading: Living expenses, utilities, and internet
s4.body: We'll cover rent or mortgage, utilities, internet, and other regular housing costs. Local property charges and one-off fees can go here too so nothing is left out of your monthly picture.
s4.illustrationA11y: Illustration of an apartment building with windows

housingStatus.title: Do you rent, own, or live with family?
housingStatus.helper: Choose what best describes your current situation.
housingStatus.illustrationA11y: Illustration of people moving into an apartment

rentDetails.title: How much is your monthly rent?
rentDetails.helper: Enter base rent only — As long as the utilities amount is known, you'll enter them separately next. If you don't know the utilities amount (dorm, all-inclusive, etc.), just enter the total and skip utilities in the next section.

rentUtilities.title: How much do you pay for utilities?
rentUtilities.helper: Gas, electricity, water — combined. An estimate is fine. Skip if you don't know or prefer not to separate.
rentUtilities.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a person printing household utility invoices
rentUtilities.selectHelper: Browse categories and tap the utilities you pay for separately. You will enter amounts on the next step.
rentUtilities.fillHelper: Enter the cost and payment schedule for each utility you selected.
rentUtilities.selectPrompt: Tap the utilities you pay for separately.
rentUtilities.skip: Included with rent or amount not known
rentUtilities.modeLabel: How would you like to enter utilities?
rentUtilities.validationAmount: Please enter the cost for each utility
rentUtilities.validationCustomName: Please enter a name for custom utilities

housingUtilities.title: Do you pay for internet separately?
housingUtilities.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a person with a Wi-Fi signal and connected devices
housingUtilities.helper: Skip if internet is included in your rent or utilities.

mortgageStatus.title: Do you have a mortgage?
mortgageStatus.helper: Select 'No' if you own the property outright.
mortgageStatus.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a property agreement and house keys

mortgageDetails.title: How much is your monthly mortgage payment?
mortgageDetails.helper: Total monthly amount including principal and interest.

ownershipCosts.title: Any other regular home ownership costs?
ownershipCosts.helper: Maintenance fund, building insurance, service charge, property tax — anything recurring.

govtTaxes.title: Government and city charges
govtTaxes.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a person reviewing tax and government documents
govtTaxes.helper: Most households pay fixed statutory charges. We've pre-filled the most common ones — confirm or adjust.
govtTaxes.wasteTaxNoteEstimated: {{amount}}/yr — estimated for {{summary}}. Municipal rules vary; edit if your charge differs.
govtTaxes.dontPayTvLicence: I don't pay TV licence fees
govtTaxes.dontPayRadioLicence: I don't pay Radio licence fees

familyHousing.title: Do you contribute to household costs?
familyHousing.helper: Contributing to rent, groceries, bills — add any regular support.
familyHousing.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a person reviewing an invoice and household bills

s5.heading: Transportation and getting around
s5.body: Tell us about cars, fuel, insurance, parking, and public transport for your household. We'll turn these into monthly amounts so transport doesn't get overlooked in your fixed costs.
s5.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a bus driver at a bus stop

motorcycle.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a person riding a motorcycle on the road

passenger.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a person driving a car

bicycle.title: Bicycle maintenance
bicycle.helper: Add any planned bicycle maintenance or repairs you're saving for — like a new chain, brake pads, or a flat tyre fix.

publicTransport.title: Public transport
publicTransport.helper: Enter your regular public transport costs — skip if you don't use public transport.
publicTransport.skip: Skip — I don't use public transport
publicTransport.validation: Please enter the public transport cost

vehicleOwnership.title: Do you have a car or other vehicle?
vehicleOwnership.helper: Motorcycle, scooter, van — anything with an engine that you use regularly.
vehicleOwnership.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a person presenting a car at a dealership

vehicleCounts.title: How many vehicles do you have?
vehicleCounts.helper: Tell us how many vehicles you own across each category.
vehicleCounts.validation: Please add at least one vehicle

vehicleFuel.title: Fuel and running costs
vehicleFuel.helper: What type of fuel does your vehicle use, and roughly how much do you spend per month?
vehicleFuel.displayNameHelper: Optional — shown in your commitments list.
vehicleFuel.validation: Please enter your monthly fuel cost

vehicleInsurance.title: Vehicle insurance
vehicleInsurance.helper: Enter your insurance details — skip if you don't have separate vehicle insurance.
vehicleInsurance.helperMandatory: Enter your insurance details — liability insurance is mandatory for passenger vehicles.
vehicleInsurance.displayNameHelper: Optional — shown in your commitments list.
vehicleInsurance.liabilityAmountHelper: This coverage limit is tracked as a financial risk, not included in your monthly budget.

vehicleMaintenance.title: Zone parking or resident permits
vehicleMaintenance.helper: If you pay for zone parking or a resident permit in your city, enter the cost here.

vehicleService.title: MOT / STK and maintenance
vehicleService.helper: When does your MOT/STK expire? Add any planned maintenance items you're saving for.
vehicleService.nextMotHelper: Auto-filled to {{years}} years after the expiry date above — adjust if needed.
vehicleService.vignetteHelper: We will open a savings tab so you can set money aside before it expires.

s6.heading: Health Insurance
s6.body: We'll ask how health coverage is arranged for you, your partner, and each child, along with monthly premiums. This keeps insurance alongside your other fixed costs in one place.

health.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a person with health insurance documents and a shield
health.title: Health insurance for {{name}}
health.helper: Confirm how {{name}}'s health insurance is handled.
health.coveredByEmployerNote: In the Czech Republic, employers pay health insurance for their employees. No action needed.
health.renewalPlanLabel: When this contract ends, what do you plan to do?
health.renewalRenewHelper: Same coverage, same contract period, and the same total premium — we'll help you save for that payment.
health.premiumPaidInFullLabel: Was this contract period paid in full upfront?
health.premiumPaidInFullHelper: If you paid the full premium before the start date, we won't deduct it monthly now — we'll help you save for renewal instead.
health.renewReserveSummary: Save {{amount}} per month for {{months}} months to cover {{total}} at renewal.
health.renewReserveExplain: Your current period is already paid. This monthly reserve builds up toward the next payment.
health.renewReserveTightWarning: Renewal is only {{months}} months away and you may not have enough saved yet (about {{shortfall}} still needed). You can use a lower monthly amount if the suggested one feels too high.
health.budgetForRenewLabel: Include this monthly reserve in your budget?
health.budgetForRenewHelper: We'll deduct this from your daily spending allowance until the contract ends.
health.renewUseCustomHelper: Choose this if the suggested amount is too high for your budget right now.
health.renewalSwitchHelper: Different coverage, payment frequency, or price when this contract ends — enter your best estimate.
health.renewalEndHelper: You don't plan to continue this insurance after it expires.
health.switchPlanIntro: Estimate what you'll pay for the new policy. Prices can go up or down before you sign — you can update this later.
health.switchPremiumHelper: Total amount you expect to pay when the new policy starts (for the period shown below).
health.switchPremiumFrequencyHelper: How you expect to pay after the new policy starts. We spread the amount above over the months until your current contract ends.
health.validation: Please confirm this member's insurance details

healthCoverage.title: Health insurance for {{name}}
healthCoverage.helper: Confirm how {{name}}'s health insurance is handled.
healthCoverage.coveredByEmployerNote: In the Czech Republic, employers pay health insurance for their employees. No action needed.
healthCoverage.validation: Please confirm this member's insurance details

s7.heading: Children's Expenses
s7.body: For each child we'll capture regular expenses — childcare, school, activities, clothing, and anything else that repeats each month. You can add costs per child so the totals stay accurate.

childrenCosts.illustrationA11y: Illustration of children preparing their backpacks for school

childrenCosts.title: Costs for {{name}}
childrenCosts.helper: Browse categories for {{name}}'s age group ({{ageGroup}}). Tap costs to add — you will enter amounts on the next step.
childrenCosts.fillHelper: Enter the amount and frequency for each cost you selected for {{name}}.
childrenCosts.validation: Please enter the amount for each cost

field.diapersHelper: Monthly cost of diapers, wipes, creams
field.formulaHelper: Monthly cost of formula and baby food
field.clothingHelper: Monthly average for clothing and shoes
field.toysHelper: Monthly average for toys, playgroups, activities
field.schoolSuppliesHelper: Monthly average for school supplies and materials
field.allowanceHelper: Monthly allowance given to the child
field.transportHelper: Monthly school transport or bus pass costs
field.extracurricularHelper: Sports, music lessons, tutoring — monthly average
field.savingsHelper: Monthly savings set aside for the child

s8.heading: Pets
s8.body: If you have pets, we'll record each one's type plus food, vet care, insurance, and any local pet taxes. Even occasional vet visits can be averaged into your monthly plan.

pets.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a person with their happy dog
pets.formIllustrationA11y: Illustration of pets dressed in Halloween costumes

hasPets.title: Do you have any pets?
hasPets.helper: Dogs, cats, birds, fish, rabbits — any animal you care for regularly.

petDetails.heading: Your pets
petDetails.title: Pet {{n}}
petDetails.dogTaxHelper: In the Czech Republic, dog owners pay an annual dog tax. Pre-filled at 1 500 Kč.

s9.heading: Subscriptions
s9.body: We'll list streaming, apps, cloud storage, and other digital subscriptions you pay for regularly. Small recurring charges add up — capturing them here keeps your fixed costs honest.

subscriptions.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a person holding a credit card
subscriptions.formIllustrationA11y: Illustration of a person entering payment details on a phone

serviceSelection.title: What subscriptions do you have?
serviceSelection.helper: Browse categories and tap the services you pay for. You will enter amounts on the next step.
serviceSelection.fillHelper: Enter the cost and payment schedule for each service in this category.
serviceSelection.chargeDayRequired: Please select a payment day for each subscription
serviceSelection.endDateRequired: End date is required when auto-renew is off
serviceSelection.categoryRequired: Please choose a category for each item
serviceSelection.customNameRequired: Please enter a name for custom subscriptions
serviceSelection.validation: Please enter the cost for each subscription
serviceSelection.osvcNote: As a self-employed person (OSVČ), include your social and health insurance under Legal items.
serviceSelection.streamingFlag: You have {{count}} streaming subscriptions costing {{amount}}/month total.

s10.heading: Everyday / variable spending
s10.body: Groceries, hair salon, laundry, and other regular spending that varies month to month belong here — enter your average monthly amount, not individual purchases. Subscriptions and fixed bills are captured in earlier sections.

costSelection.title: What everyday or variable costs do you have?
costSelection.helper: Tap common items you pay regularly, or add custom ones below. Enter your typical monthly average.
costSelection.validation: Please enter the amount for each cost
costSelection.osvcNote: As a self-employed person (OSVČ), don't forget to include your social and health insurance contributions.

s11.heading: Debts & Loans
s11.body: We'll capture loans and credit balances with monthly repayments and interest rates. Knowing what goes out toward debt each month is essential before we calculate your flexible spending allowance.

debts.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a person stressed about money and debt
debts.formIllustrationA11y: Illustration of a person managing money stress with bills and a piggy bank

hasDebts.title: Do you have any debts or loans?
hasDebts.helper: Credit cards, personal loans, student loans, BNPL — anything you're currently paying off.

debtDetails.title: Your debts
debtDetails.helper: Enter the details of this debt.
debtDetails.minPaymentHelper: The minimum amount you need to pay each month to stay in good standing.
debtDetails.aprHelper: Annual Percentage Rate — the yearly interest rate on this debt.
debtDetails.highAprWarning: ⚠️ This APR is above 20%. Consider prioritising this debt.

s12.heading: Set your flexible spending budget
s12.body: Here you'll set your flexible monthly spending allowance — what's left after fixed costs and debt — and choose how unspent budget carries forward. This is the number you'll check day to day.

budget.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a person using a finance app on their phone
budget.formIllustrationA11y: Illustration of a person managing their budget with a finance app

budgetSplit.title: Your flexible spending budget
budgetSplit.helper: This is what's left after fixed costs, debt payments, and savings. You can adjust it if needed — on the next step you'll choose how unspent money rolls over.
budgetSplit.validation: Please enter your flexible budget amount
budgetSplit.displayFrequencyLabel: How you want to see your budget
budgetSplit.displayHelper: Summary stays monthly; preview uses your chosen period.

splitSlider.label: Adjust spending vs savings
splitSlider.helper: Slide left to lower your budget and send more to savings each month. Slide right to spend more.

budgetSplit.loadError: We could not load your household data. Check your connection and try again.

deductSavingsGoal.label: Reserve savings goal from spending budget?
deductSavingsGoal.helper: When enabled, your spending preview subtracts the monthly amount needed to reach your savings goal.
deductSavingsGoal.previewNote: {{deduction}} reserved for your goal · {{amount}} to spend

rollover.title: What happens at month end?
rollover.helperMonthly: When the calendar month closes, any flexible budget you have not spent can roll over or reset.
rollover.helper: When the calendar month closes, any flexible budget you have not spent can roll over or reset.
rollover.freeDesc: Any money you do not spend is added to next month's flexible budget — your daily allowance goes up until you use it.
rollover.cappedDesc: Unspent budget carries over, but only up to a limit you set — overflow goes to loose cash.
rollover.resetDesc: Each month starts fresh. Unspent budget does not roll into next month's spending allowance — it goes to the destination you choose below.
rollover.capTypeMultiplierHelper: e.g. ×2 means rollover can reach twice your monthly flexible budget.
rollover.capTypeAmountHelper: Set a maximum rollover balance in currency — e.g. 5 000 Kč.
rollover.resetDestinationHelper: At month end, leftover flexible budget is routed to a tracked balance — so your numbers still add up.
rollover.resetPiggyBankHelper: Leftover is recorded in your piggy bank — not added to next month's spending allowance or formal savings.
rollover.resetForfeitHelper: Leftover is recorded as loose cash — not added to next month's spending allowance or formal savings.
rollover.resetToSavingsHelper: Leftover budget is moved into your savings balance at the end of each month.
rollover.resetToOtherGoalHelper: Track it separately — e.g. holiday fund, car repair, or a one-off purchase.
rollover.validationCapAmount: Please enter a custom rollover cap amount
rollover.validationOtherGoal: Please name the goal you are setting money aside for

spendingStrategy.title: What happens to unspent daily allowance?
spendingStrategy.helper: When a day ends, any allowance you did not use can stay in your spending pool, go to your piggy bank, or move to savings. This applies within each month or pay cycle.
spendingStrategy.spendingBoostDesc: Leftover stays available for later days in this month or cycle — your daily allowance can rise as you underspend.
spendingStrategy.looseMoneyDesc: Move unspent allowance to your piggy bank — spend anytime, not added to tomorrow's allowance.
spendingStrategy.savingsDesc: Add unspent allowance to your savings balance at the end of each completed day.
spendingStrategy.validation: Please choose what happens to unspent daily allowance

s13.heading: Check everything looks right
s13.body: You'll see a full summary of everything you entered, organized by section. Take a moment to check each part before we build your household budget.

review.illustrationA11y: Illustration of a person confirming their information with a checkmark
review.formIllustrationA11y: Illustration of a person reviewing and confirming their household budget details

review.title: Review your information
review.helper: Expand any section to check or edit details before continuing.
review.editSubsectionHelper: Update line items below — changes save when you tap Save on each row.
review.editSubsectionEmpty: Nothing to edit in this section yet.

alerts.zeroIncome: Income appears to be 0 — make sure you've completed the Income & Savings section.
alerts.zeroIncomeHint: No income recorded yet — add your income sources so your balance is accurate.

labels.goalIntent: What do you want Beaverr to help with?

dashboard.title: Dashboard

tabRoles.dashboard: Your monthly plan and what needs attention.
tabRoles.income: Household income and savings goals — edit amounts inline.
tabRoles.expenses: Fixed costs and debt payments already committed each month.
tabRoles.budget: How much is left for everyday spending after commitments.
tabRoles.profile: Your account name and username from sign-up.
tabRoles.subscriptions: Your plan, billing, and PRO features.
tabRoles.accountSettings: Country, language, currency, and delete account.
tabRoles.helpFeedback: Help articles and feedback for the Beaverr team.
tabRoles.summary: Full ledger with health metrics and section-by-section analysis.

subscriptionsScreen.helper: Subscription and billing management is coming soon. You are on the free plan for now.

accountSettingsScreen.helper: Manage your account preferences and data.
accountSettingsScreen.privacyHelper: Clear household data from this device and the cloud, or permanently delete your Beaverr account.
accountSettingsScreen.clearSavedDataConfirmMessage: This removes your household progress on this device and in the cloud. Your Beaverr account stays active.
accountSettingsScreen.clearSavedDataFailed: Could not clear saved data. Please try again.
accountSettingsScreen.revokeAiConsentConfirmMessage: AI insights will stop using your anonymized household data. You can enable them again from any dashboard tab.
accountSettingsScreen.preferencesHelper: Update country, language, and currency from when you signed up.

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.helper: Choose how Beaverr looks on this device. Your choice is saved automatically.
appearance.lightDescription: Cool paper background with navy text — best in bright rooms.
appearance.darkDescription: Deep navy canvas with softened blues — easier on the eyes at night.

helpFeedbackScreen.helper: Help articles and in-app feedback are coming soon. For now, contact support through your app store listing.

profileScreen.helper: Manage your Beaverr profile and preferences here.
profileScreen.accountHelper: Update the name and username you entered when you signed up.

headerToolbar.logOutConfirmMessage: This will delete all your data and progress. You will need to set up Beaverr again.

ledgerStrip.toSpendNote: {{spend}} of {{available}} available set aside for everyday spending
ledgerStrip.a11y: Monthly plan: income, committed costs, available after commitments, spending budget

ledgerCascade.committedHint: Includes bills, subscriptions and debt payments
ledgerCascade.savedIncluded: Not separated from spending — enable deduct on Budget to protect this amount
ledgerCascade.costsReducedNone: No reduction yet — edit expenses to lower committed costs
ledgerCascade.toSpendIncludedGoal: Your savings goal pace is included in this amount
ledgerCascade.a11y: Monthly plan: income, committed costs, available, savings, spending budget, unallocated cash

toast.cycleCalendarLocked: Day logging unlocks during an active pay cycle
toast.stashTransferEmpty: Nothing to move — balance is zero
toast.commitmentRenewed: Commitment reset for the next due date

ledgerTable.deleteConfirmMessage: {{label}} will be removed from your budget.

saveFeedback.updated: Spending budget is now {{amount}} per month ({{delta}})
saveFeedback.unchanged: Saved. Spending budget unchanged at {{amount}} per month.

dashboard.tabComingSoon: This section is coming in a future update.

errorBoundary.title: Something went wrong
errorBoundary.body: We couldn't display the dashboard. Try again or refresh the app.

metricExplain.tapHint: Tap to see how this is calculated

flexibleBudget.title: Your spending budget
flexibleBudget.meaning: Money left for everyday spending after rent, bills, and debt payments. Toggle daily, weekly, or monthly to see it in the period that suits you.
flexibleBudget.meaningDeducted: Your spending budget after reserving the monthly amount needed for your savings goal. Toggle daily, weekly, or monthly to see it in the period that suits you.

fixedLoad.title: Fixed load
fixedLoad.meaning: This shows how much of your income is already spoken for by fixed costs and minimum debt payments. Above 80% feels tight; above 100% means commitments exceed income.

monthlyBuffer.title: Budget flexibility
monthlyBuffer.meaning: Cash left over after your spending budget is set aside. Think of it as wiggle room — positive means extra headroom; negative means the spending budget is higher than what you actually have left.
monthlyBuffer.formula: Income − fixed costs − debt payments − spending budget

recurringCosts.title: Recurring costs
recurringCosts.meaning: Subscriptions, insurance premiums, and debt minimum payments that repeat every month. These are the costs you can review when you want to free up money.

totalIncome.title: Total income
totalIncome.meaning: The sum of all household income converted to a monthly amount — salaries, benefits, and any other regular sources you entered during setup.
totalIncome.formula: Each income source converted to monthly, then added together

totalExpenses.title: Committed costs
totalExpenses.meaning: Fixed household costs plus minimum debt payments due each month. This is money already committed before everyday spending.

cashAfterBills.title: Left after bills
cashAfterBills.meaning: What remains from your income after fixed costs and debt payments — before you plan everyday spending or savings.

savingsGoalPlan.title: Saving goal
savingsGoalPlan.targetMeaning: The amount you want to save and the deadline you set during setup. We use this to work out how much to set aside each month.
savingsGoalPlan.ongoingMeaning: A fixed amount you plan to set aside every month with no end date — useful for building savings steadily over time.
savingsGoalPlan.noGoalMeaning: You have not set a savings goal yet. Add one in Income to see your target here.
savingsGoalPlan.reduceCostsMeaning: Your primary goal is to lower household spending rather than save toward a specific amount.

savingsGoal.title: Goal tracking
savingsGoal.meaning: Shows how much you have saved so far and how much to set aside per day, week, or month to reach your target by the deadline.
savingsGoal.ongoingMeaning: Shows your total saved balance and how much you plan to set aside on an ongoing basis. Toggle daily, weekly, or monthly to see the pace in the period that suits you.
savingsGoal.noGoalMeaning: Set a savings goal in Income to see how much you need to put aside and track your progress here.

income.summary: Page amounts per day, week, or month.
income.detail: Your income is stored as monthly figures. Daily and weekly views divide those totals for display only — they do not change what you entered during setup.
income.infoA11y: Learn how display frequency affects Income

expenses.summary: Page amounts per day, week, or month.
expenses.detail: Costs are stored as monthly commitments. Daily and weekly views recalculate display only. Fixed load and recurring percentages stay the same.
expenses.infoA11y: Learn how display frequency affects Expenses

budget.summary: Budget total per day, week, or month.
budget.detail: Your budget is planned monthly. Daily and weekly views show an equivalent allowance. Sliders and settings below stay in monthly terms.
budget.infoA11y: Learn how display frequency affects Budget

home.summary: To spend and saved-so-far amounts per day, week, or month.
home.detail: Your plan is stored monthly. Daily and weekly views divide totals for display only — they do not change your underlying budget.
home.infoA11y: Learn how display frequency affects the dashboard

spendingPace.good: On plan — spending matches the time left in this period.
spendingPace.alert: Slightly ahead of plan — worth watching discretionary spend.
spendingPace.important: Ahead of plan — ease spending to stay within budget.
spendingPace.critical: Budget nearly depleted — pause discretionary spending.
spendingPace.backfillPending: Finish logging past days for an accurate spending pace.
spendingPace.backfillPendingNote: Finish logging past days for a fully accurate pace.
spendingPace.backfillPendingA11y: Finish logging past days for an accurate spending pace.
spendingPace.detail: {{spentPct}} of cycle budget spent · {{timePct}} of period elapsed
spendingPace.line: {{summary}} · {{spentPct}} of cycle budget spent · {{timePct}} of period elapsed

hero.setGoalPrompt: Set a goal to track your monthly savings plan

monthlyPlan.title: Your monthly plan
monthlyPlan.helper: Income, fixed costs, and what's left to spend or save this month.

burnRate.title: Where your income goes
burnRate.incomeBase: Total income split across committed costs, savings, and spending.

savedSoFar.title: Saved so far
savedSoFar.dailyNote: Today's unspent allowance stays in your spending pool or routes at day end based on your policy.
savedSoFar.infoA11y: How saved so far is calculated

explain.meaningBody: Money you have effectively set aside this month — from completed days' underspend plus your savings plan for elapsed time.
explain.formula: Planned savings uses {{days}} of {{totalDays}} days elapsed. Today's allowance is not included until the day ends.

daily.spendingBoost: Daily underspend stays in your spending pool.
daily.looseMoney: Daily underspend goes to your piggy bank.
daily.savings: Daily underspend is added to savings.

monthly.free: Month-end leftovers boost next month's spending.
monthly.capped: Month-end leftovers fill the rollover jar up to your limit.
monthly.resetSavings: Month-end leftovers are added to savings.
monthly.resetLoose: Month-end leftovers go to your piggy bank.

strategy.free: Daily underspend goes to your daily jar. Month-end leftovers boost next month's spending.
strategy.capped: Daily underspend fills your daily jar (with cap). Month-end leftovers fill the rollover jar up to your limit.
strategy.resetSavings: Daily underspend goes to your daily jar. Month-end leftovers are added to savings.
strategy.resetLoose: Daily underspend goes to your daily jar. Month-end leftovers go to loose cash.
strategy.resetOther: Daily underspend goes to your daily jar. Month-end leftovers go to {{name}}.

activity.title: Daily jar
activity.helper: Unspent daily allowance from completed days — for outings, treats, and small plans.
activity.helperLegacy: Balance from when daily underspend went to this jar — change policy in Budget settings.
activity.helperCapped: Fills from completed days up to {{cap}}. Overflow goes to loose cash.
activity.helperFull: Jar is full at {{cap}}. Further daily underspend goes to loose cash.

rollover.title: Rollover jar
rollover.helperFree: Month-end unspent budget — increases next month's flexible allowance.
rollover.helperCappedFull: Full at {{cap}}. Extra month-end unspent goes to loose cash.

looseCash.title: Piggy bank
looseCash.helper: Set-aside from day-end underspend and month-end reset — use anytime, not added to next month's allowance.

bigPlans.title: {{name}}
bigPlans.helper: Month-end unspent budget set aside for larger plans like travel or one-off purchases.

savings.title: Savings
savings.helper: Month-end unspent flexible budget is moved here at month end.

home.spendingBudgetNoteDeducted: {{spend}} to spend · {{goal}} reserved for your goal

goalTracking.title: Goal tracking
goalTracking.ongoingLabel: Ongoing amount set aside each month
goalTracking.monthlyNeededLabel: Monthly amount needed to reach your goal
goalTracking.savePaceLabel: Save {{amount}} per {{period}} to reach your goal

home.monthlyNote: {{amount}} per month after fixed costs and debt payments
home.loadError: We could not load your household data.
home.emptySubtitle: Complete setup to see your household budget here.

health.fixedLoadLabel: Of income committed to fixed costs and debt
health.recurringLabel: Share of income for recurring bills

trackerSnapshot.title: Spending pace
trackerSnapshot.noLogs: No spending logged yet — your full allowance is available.
trackerSnapshot.noLogsDaily: No spending logged today — your full daily allowance is available.
trackerSnapshot.noLogsWeekly: No spending logged this week — your full weekly allowance is available.
trackerSnapshot.a11y: Spending pace today and this week, open tracker

dailySpend.title: Log today's spending
dailySpend.helper: Enter the total you spent today on groceries, dining out, and other flexible costs.
dailySpend.openFormA11y: Open form to log today's flexible spending
dailySpend.validation: Enter a valid amount (0 or more).

monthEndStatus.title: Month-end routing
monthEndStatus.a11y: Month-end routing status, open budget settings

recurring.title: Recurring commitments
recurring.subBudgetHeavy: Subscriptions use {{pct}}% of your flexible budget

kpi.committedCostsHint: Fixed costs + debt payments per month

chart.expensesSubtitle: Fixed costs by category — tap a row to see line items

insights.empty: No insight available for this view yet.
insights.emptyTracker: Log daily spending to unlock tracker insights.
insights.emptyGoals: Add a goal to unlock goal insights.
insights.emptySavings: Record savings to unlock stash insights.
insights.emptyAlerts: No active reminders — insights appear when alerts need attention.
insights.emptyIncome: Add income sources to unlock income insights.
insights.emptyExpenses: Record expenses to unlock cost insights.
insights.emptyBudget: Set up your flexible budget to unlock budget insights.
insights.emptySummary: Complete a pay cycle to unlock summary insights.

headline.balanced: Your fixed costs and flexible budget look balanced.
headline.tight: Fixed costs use {{pct}}% of income — review your largest categories.
headline.overcommitted: Fixed costs and debt payments exceed {{pct}}% of income.
headline.topCategory: {{category}} is your largest cost at {{pct}}% of income.

housing.highShare: Housing takes {{pct}}% of income — worth reviewing if you can reduce it.

subscriptions.summary: {{count}} subscriptions cost {{pct}}% of income each month.
subscriptions.manyStreaming: {{count}} streaming services — you may be able to consolidate.
subscriptions.renewalsSoon: {{count}} renewals coming up in the next 30 days.

other.empty: No everyday or variable spending recorded.

debts.summary: {{count}} debts with minimum payments each month.
debts.highApr: {{count}} high-interest debt(s) — extra payments will help most.

budget.summary: Flexible spending is {{pct}}% of income after fixed costs.
budget.summaryLead: Your flexible spending is {{pct}}% of income — within a healthy range for everyday costs.
budget.summaryLow: Your flexible spending is {{pct}}% of income — below the recommended 20–30% for a household your size.
budget.detailTopCategory: Consider reviewing fixed costs — {{category}} alone is {{amount}}/mo and could be optimised.
budget.detailGeneric: Review your largest cost categories if you want more room for everyday spending.
budget.negativeSurplus: Your flexible budget is higher than what's left after fixed costs.
budget.negativeSurplusLead: Your flexible budget exceeds what's left after fixed costs.
budget.negativeSurplusDetail: Lower spending or review fixed costs so the numbers add up.

goals.onTrack: Your goal looks achievable with your current flexible budget.
goals.atRisk: You may need to save more each month or adjust your goal date.
goals.portfolioBehind: {{count}} goal(s) need attention — check pace on the Goals tab.

savings.empty: No savings recorded yet — money you set aside will show up here.
savings.summary: You have {{amount}} saved across your stash tabs.
savings.withInflow: You have {{amount}} saved. About {{inflow}}/mo is planned to flow in from your budget.

tracker.empty: Log daily spending to see how you are tracking against your budget.
tracker.needsLogging: {{count}} days still need logging — finish them for an accurate spending pace.
tracker.onTrack: Your spending is logged and tracking looks up to date.

alerts.empty: No active reminders or alerts right now.

generic.summary: This category is {{pct}}% of your monthly income.

advice.title: Personalized advice
advice.disclaimer: AI-generated guidance for education only — not financial, legal, or tax advice.
advice.error: Could not load personalized advice. Try again later.
advice.notConfigured: Cloud advice is not configured on this build.
advice.consentLabel: Send anonymized household totals to Google Cloud for personalized explanations
advice.consentHelper: Google processes data as a subprocessor under their cloud terms. No names or account numbers are sent. Follow-up questions in chat use the same anonymized totals.
advice.signInTitle: Sign in to get personalized advice
advice.signInHelper: Your account links advice to you and enables secure access.
advice.signInError: Sign-in failed. Check your email and password.

chat.title: Ask about your insight
chat.askAboutThisA11y: Ask a follow-up question about this insight
chat.historyEmpty: No previous chats yet. Start a conversation to see it here.
chat.historyDeleteMessage: This conversation will be permanently removed from your history.
chat.error: Could not send your message. Try again.
chat.emptyHint: Ask a follow-up about the numbers on this tab. Official Czech resources may appear as links below replies.
chat.emptyHintGeneral: Ask anything about your household budget, spending, or money goals.
chat.emptyHintInsight: Ask a follow-up about this insight. Official Czech resources may appear as links below replies.

summaryScreen.intro: Full household ledger with insights on each section. Tap Edit to update any area.
summaryScreen.risksNote: Exposure amounts — not included in monthly budget

cycleOverview.title: Pay cycles
cycleOverview.helper: How many spending periods you have tracked and how closed ones ended.
cycleOverview.empty: No pay cycles yet. When you start a pay cycle, it will count here.
cycleOverview.cyclesDisabled: Pay cycles are off. Turn them on in Budget to track spending periods.

cycleHistory.title: Historic cycles
cycleHistory.empty: No closed cycles yet. When you end a pay cycle, it will appear here.
cycleHistory.cyclesDisabled: Pay cycles are off — no cycle history to show yet.

dailySpend.title: Daily spending
dailySpend.helper: Each column is one day. Navy is spent; grey is saved on past days or remaining today; red extends above budget when over.
dailySpend.empty: No days in this month yet.

cashAfterBills.label: Left after bills
cashAfterBills.footer: After fixed costs and debt payments

fixedLoad.label: Bills vs income
fixedLoad.footer: Share of income for bills and debt

recurring.label: Regular bills
recurring.footer: Share of income for subscriptions and similar

expensesScreen.emptyPanel: No expenses recorded in this group yet.
expensesScreen.emptyHint: Use Add expense above to record it here.

tabs.addA11y: Add a new expense — choose category first

helper.overview: All committed costs by category — rent, bills, debt payments, and more.
helper.fixed: Costs that stay roughly the same each month — rent, utilities, insurance, taxes.
helper.recurring: Regular but variable bills — subscriptions, transport, pets, and minimum debt payments.

addPicker.title: What are you adding?
addPicker.helper: Pick a category — we'll open the right form for that expense type.

expensesScreen.subtabEmpty: You don't have any {{type}} expenses yet.

obligations.title: Cycle payback obligations
obligations.helper: Shortfalls covered from outside your budget when a pay cycle closed. These are separate from your regular debt list.

dates.optionalHint: Optional — helps show end dates and upcoming payments in your expense tables.

expensesScreen.editSectionHint: This expense is part of a household section. Open the section editor to change details.

fixedRecurring.title: Fixed and recurring expenses
fixedRecurring.salaryImpact: {{amount}} of your {{income}} monthly income ({{pct}}%) goes to fixed and recurring expenses

goalsScreen.empty: You have not set a financial goal yet.

hero.noPaceHelper: Set a deadline to track pace

goalsScreen.emptyActive: No active goals yet. Add a goal or complete onboarding to see auto-created ones.

reset.disabledA11y: Reset unavailable — {{name}} has no progress yet
reset.confirmBody: This sets {{name}} back to zero saved. Linked tabs and funding rules stay in place.
reset.confirmBodyDebt: This sets {{name}} back to the starting balance. Linked tabs and funding rules stay in place.
reset.error: Progress could not be reset. Try again.

goalsScreen.clarityHelper: You are building a clear picture of where your money goes. Explore your dashboard and budget breakdown anytime.
goalsScreen.reduceCostsHelper: Beaverr will focus on finding savings and trimming spending.
goalsScreen.reduceCostsTap: Tap to open your cost reduction tracker.
goalsScreen.ongoingHelper: A fixed amount you plan to set aside each month.

deadlineMode.helper: Without a deadline, only current and target amounts are shown — no progress bar.

goalsScreen.onTrack: On track — you have {{amount}} flexible each month.
goalsScreen.gap: You need {{needed}} per month but only have {{available}} flexible.

create.title: New goal
create.savingsHelper: Set a target amount and optional deadline.
create.debtHelper: Pick a debt from your plan or add a new one to track.
create.validationKind: Choose saving or debt payoff to continue.

kind.savingsHelper: Build a fund for a purchase, trip, or milestone.
kind.debtHelper: Track progress toward clearing a balance.

create.validationDebtName: Enter a name for this debt.
create.validationDebtBalance: Enter a balance greater than zero.
create.noDebtsAvailable: All debts in your plan already have payoff goals, or none are set up yet.
create.validationDebt: Choose a debt to pay off.
create.name: Name
create.validationTarget: Enter a target amount greater than zero.
create.saveError: Could not save goal. Try again.

edit.title: Edit goal
edit.helper: Change the name, deadline, or target amount.
edit.autoNameHelper: We suggested this name when the goal was created — change anything here.
edit.saveError: Could not save changes. Try again.

setDeadline.title: Goal deadline
setDeadline.subtitle: Choose whether {{name}} has a target date.
setDeadline.helper: Pick when you want to reach this goal.
setDeadline.saveError: Could not save deadline. Try again.

detail.noFunding: No tabs linked yet — open the goal card and add a funding link.
detail.noMovements: No contributions recorded yet — they appear when linked tabs transfer money to this goal.

alerts.onHold: {{name}} is on hold because the linked debt was removed.

funding.title: Linking goal
funding.subtitle: Link {{name}} to a money stash tab.
funding.addNewLinkA11y: Add another stash link for this goal
funding.startDateHelper: Money moves on this date and then repeats. Earliest is tomorrow.
funding.linkKindPrompt: Is this a one-time move or a regular link?

linkKind.onceHelper: Move money once — now or on a chosen date.
linkKind.recurringHelper: Repeat daily, weekly, monthly, or yearly from a start date.

funding.oneTimeDateHelper: Money moves once on this date.
funding.oneTimeImmediateHelper: Money moves from the tab as soon as you save.
funding.saveError: Could not save link. Try again.
funding.validationTab: Choose a money tab to link.
funding.validationAmount: Enter an amount greater than zero.
funding.validationDateFuture: First move must be tomorrow or later.
funding.validationDateToday: Move date cannot be in the past.
funding.validationEmptySource: That tab has no money to move.
funding.validationInsufficient: Could not move that amount from the tab.
funding.validationGoalAtTarget: This goal has already reached its target amount.
funding.validationExceedsTarget: Enter {{remaining}} or less — that's what's left to reach your goal.
funding.validationDuplicate: You already have a link from this tab at this frequency.

reduceCostsScreen.title: Cost reduction
reduceCostsScreen.baselineHelper: Monthly committed costs when you started tracking reductions.
reduceCostsScreen.placeholder: Detailed charts and comparisons coming soon.

totalBalanceExplain.infoA11y: How total money saved is calculated
totalBalanceExplain.meaningBody: The combined balance across every tab in Your money stash — piggy bank, savings, and any custom tabs you have created.
totalBalanceExplain.formula: Each tab balance added together = total saved today.

expectedSavingsExplain.meaningBody: Your projected total across all money stash tabs at 31 December {{year}}, assuming you keep saving at your planned monthly rate from your budget split and savings goal.
expectedSavingsExplain.formula: Current total + (planned monthly savings × {{months}} months remaining) = expected year-end total. Month-end rollover to savings is not included because it depends on actual spending.
expectedSavingsExplain.noPlanWarning: No monthly savings are planned yet — set a budget split or savings goal to see a meaningful projection.
expectedSavingsExplain.cappedAtGoalWarning: Projection stops at your savings goal target — you may reach it before year end.

detail.noLinkedGoals: No goals pull money from this tab yet.
detail.uncommittedHelper: Nothing in this tab is reserved until a scheduled transfer runs or you move money manually.
detail.noMovements: No movements yet — transfers, goal links, and budget routing will appear here.

burnRate.title: Where this money goes
burnRate.helper: Split of what moved out of this tab and what is still here.
burnRate.empty: Add a balance or record a movement to see where money goes.
burnRate.plannedNote: Planned monthly outflow to linked goals: {{amount}} — nothing has left this tab yet.

commitments.renewA11y: Start saving for the next {{name}} due date
commitments.deleteBody: Deleting {{name}} removes its linked expense from your Expenses tab and updates your budget. This cannot be undone.
commitments.renewToast: {{name}} reset for the next due date

savingsScreen.monthlyPlanHelper: From your budget split and savings goal.

monthlyPlanRolloverBanner.title: + Month rollover
monthlyPlanRolloverBanner.body: Unspent budget may add at month end depending on spending — not included above.
monthlyPlanRolloverBanner.linkA11y: Open Budget tab and highlight your month-end rollover strategy
monthlyPlanRolloverBanner.a11y: Month-end rollover may add variable savings

savingsScreen.noInflows: No monthly savings planned yet — adjust your budget or set a savings goal.
savingsScreen.resetToSavings: Leftover flexible budget is added to savings at month end.
savingsScreen.resetToOther: Leftover budget is set aside for: {{name}}
savingsScreen.resetLooseMoney: Leftover flexible budget is tracked in your piggy bank at month end.
savingsScreen.looseMoneyHelper: Set-aside money from months when you underspent your flexible budget.
savingsScreen.otherGoalHelper: Money set aside at month end for this goal.

chart.empty: Add a savings balance or monthly savings plan to see a projection.

intro.title: Spending pace
intro.body: See how you're doing today, this week, and this month — plus where leftover budget will go at month end.
intro.loggingSoon: Expense logging is coming soon. Previews will update as you record spending.

trackerScreen.noSpendLogged: No spending logged yet — allowance shows your full budget for this period.

daily.title: Today
daily.helper: Your daily flexible allowance and spending so far.

status.under: Under today's pace — room to spend.

weekly.title: This week
weekly.helper: Monday–Sunday totals for your flexible budget.

status.under: Under this week's pace — you're ahead.

monthly.title: This month
monthly.helper: Month-end routing based on spending so far and your rollover settings.
monthly.goesTo: If you keep this pace, leftover goes to
monthly.noLeftover: No leftover projected — you've used your flexible budget (or more).

route.rolloverCappedSplit: {{rollover}} to rollover · {{loose}} to loose cash (over cap)

monthEndHistory.title: Past month-end routing
monthEndHistory.helper: How leftover flexible budget was routed when each month closed.

noActive.title: Start your first pay cycle
noActive.helper: Choose when money landed and how much you can spend until your next income. You can backfill days if the cycle started in the past.

backfill.bannerBody: Piggy bank: one past day at a time removes that day's allowance from your pool; unspent goes to your piggy bank. Keep in pool: logged spend reduces what's left. Bulk logging is spend-only. Daily allowance = remaining ÷ unlogged days.
backfill.bannerA11y: Catching up on past days. Piggy bank jars unspent allowance per day; keep in pool counts logged spend against what's left. Bulk logging is spend-only.

status.ok: On track — plenty of budget left.
status.deficit: Over cycle budget — funding only at close.
status.idle: No active cycle — start one when money lands.

breakdown.title: Your budget break down
breakdown.bannerPiggyBank: Unspent daily allowance from completed days was moved to your piggy bank.
breakdown.bannerSavings: Unspent daily allowance from completed days was added to savings.
breakdown.bannerGeneric: Adjustments and completed-day routing reduced your active cycle pool.

calendar.title: Cycle calendar
calendar.helper: Tap a cycle day to log or edit spending.
calendar.backfillHelper: Log each day from your cycle start through yesterday. See the note above for how spending pool and daily allowance are calculated.
calendar.lockedHelper: Day logging unlocks during an active pay cycle. Start a cycle below or from Budget.
calendar.jumpToUnsetA11y: Jump to first unlogged day in the cycle

entryTable.showAllA11y: Show all {{count}} days in the cycle

editStart.title: Change cycle start date
editStart.helper: When did this payment arrive? Moving the start adds or removes days you need to log on the calendar.
editStart.validationFuture: The start date cannot be in the future.
editStart.saveError: Could not update start date. Try again.

editDates.title: Edit cycle dates
editDates.helper: Adjust when this pay cycle started and ended. Spent, saved, and deficit are recalculated for the new range.
editDates.validationStartFuture: The start date cannot be in the future.
editDates.validationEndBeforeStart: The end date must be on or after the start date.
editDates.validationEndFuture: The end date cannot be in the future.
editDates.saveError: Could not update cycle dates. Try again.

spendLog.title: Log spending
spendLog.confirmZeroA11y: Confirm zero spending for this day

start.title: Start a pay cycle
start.paymentHelper: How much did you receive? We calculate your cycle budget from your Budget tab settings.
start.validationPayment: Enter a payment amount greater than zero.
start.backfillNotice: Your cycle starts before today — log each day from your start date through yesterday. Past days record spend only; unspent daily allowance is not moved to your piggy bank until days end naturally.
start.validationBudget: Enter a budget greater than zero.

close.title: Close pay cycle

rollover.title: Rollover strategy
rollover.helper: What happens to unspent flexible budget when the calendar month ends.
rollover.freeBody: All unspent flexible budget carries over — there is no limit on how much can roll into next month.
rollover.freeWhere: Month-end leftovers → rollover jar → boosts next month's daily allowance.
rollover.cappedBody: Unspent budget carries over, but only up to a limit you set.
rollover.cappedWhere: Month-end leftovers → rollover jar (within cap). Anything over the cap → loose cash jar.
rollover.resetBody: Each month starts fresh — unspent flexible budget does not increase next month's spending allowance.
rollover.resetWhereOptions: Month-end leftovers → piggy bank, savings, or a named goal — you choose in budget settings.
rollover.resetWherePiggyBank: Your choice: month-end leftovers → piggy bank (spend anytime, not added to next month's allowance).
rollover.resetWhereSavings: Your choice: month-end leftovers → savings balance.

dailySpending.title: Daily spending strategy
dailySpending.helper: What happens when a day ends and you have unspent allowance — within each month or pay cycle.
dailySpending.spendingBoostBody: Underspend stays in your flexible budget for later days.
dailySpending.spendingBoostWhere: Completed days → leftover stays in your active pool.
dailySpending.looseMoneyBody: Underspend moves to your piggy bank.
dailySpending.savingsBody: Underspend is added to your savings balance.
dailySpending.policyNote: Applies to completed days from tomorrow. Money already routed stays where it is.

admin.title: Admin
admin.forbiddenBody: This area is restricted to Beaverr administrators.

users.title: User management
users.deleteConfirmMessage: This permanently deletes the account and all cloud data. This cannot be undone.

knowledge.title: AI knowledge CMS
knowledge.subtitle: Manage the reference excerpts and routing rules that ground Beaverr's AI coach. Changes stay in draft until you publish.

workflow.step1: Import the bundled library (Status tab) — loads 19 book + CZ official chunks from the current JS bundle.
workflow.step2: Edit sources, chunk excerpts, and routing rules on the other tabs.
workflow.step3: Publish — marks chunks live and bumps the AI cache version so users get fresh answers.

knowledge.statusIntroBody: The app loads published chunks from the database on each AI request. Until you import and publish, it falls back to the hard-coded bundle shipped with the app.
knowledge.sourcesIntroBody: A source is a library or country pack — metadata only. Book sources (e.g. Sethi CSP, CFPB) group general finance excerpts. Country sources (e.g. cz_official) group official links for a locale. Chunks reference a source by id. Deleting a source does not delete its chunks.
knowledge.sourcesEmpty: No sources yet. Import the bundle on Overview, or add one below.
knowledge.chunksIntroBody: A chunk is a short excerpt the AI reads for grounding — never shown verbatim to users. Each chunk has an id (source#topic), belongs to a source, and can be draft or published.
knowledge.chunksEmpty: No chunks yet. Use Import JS bundle on the Overview tab first.
knowledge.chunkEditHint: Edit the excerpt text. Publish from Overview when ready to go live.
knowledge.routesIntroBody: Routing decides which chunks attach to an AI request. Use the tabs below to manage each rule type separately.

chat_keyword.title: Chat keyword routing
chat_keyword.body: When a user sends a message in Ask Beaverr, the server scans the text for matching keywords (English or Czech). If a keyword hits, the linked chunks are added to the AI context — useful for topics like rent, taxes, or visas.
chat_keyword.edit1: Click Add route and enter comma-separated keywords (e.g. rent, tenant, nájem).
chat_keyword.edit2: List chunk ids to attach when any keyword matches.
chat_keyword.edit3: Save, then Publish on Overview to go live. Edit or Delete existing rules from the list below.
chat_keyword.empty: No chat keyword routes yet. Add one to ground coach chat on specific topics.

tab.title: Tab preference routing
tab.body: Each dashboard tab (home, budget, expenses…) can prefer certain chunks even before rules fire. These are merged with rule-based and chat routes — the server picks up to 4 chunks total per request.
tab.edit1: Click Add route and enter the tab key (e.g. budget, expenses, savings).
tab.edit2: Add chunk ids in priority order — first ids are preferred.
tab.edit3: Save, then Publish on Overview. Use Edit to tweak chunk lists for a tab.
tab.empty: No tab preference routes yet. Add one to tailor AI context per dashboard tab.

rule.title: Rule trigger routing
rule.body: When evaluateAdviceRules detects a financial signal (e.g. fixed_cost_ratio_tight, savings_buffer_low), the server attaches the mapped chunks to the AI insight request. Rule ids must match the codebase exactly.
rule.edit1: Click Add route and enter the rule id from evaluateAdviceRules / evaluateTabAdviceRules.
rule.edit2: List chunk ids to attach when that rule triggers.
rule.edit3: Save, then Publish on Overview. Multiple routes can map different rules to different chunks.
rule.empty: No rule trigger routes yet. Add one to link financial rules to knowledge chunks.

routeTypeHelp.rule: Fires when evaluateAdviceRules returns this rule id (e.g. fixed_cost_ratio_tight).
routeTypeHelp.tab: Preferred chunks when the user opens this dashboard tab (home, budget, expenses…).
routeTypeHelp.chat_keyword: When the coach chat message contains any of these words (EN or CS), attach the listed chunks.

fields.sourceIdHint: Short slug, no spaces — used by chunks (e.g. sethi_csp, cz_official).
fields.title: Display title
fields.docPathHint: Link to the markdown file in the repo for editors.
fields.ruleIdHint: Must match a rule id from evaluateAdviceRules (see codebase).
fields.tabKeyHint: home, budget, expenses, savings, goals, income, tracker, alerts…
fields.keywordsHint: Lowercase words/phrases to match in coach chat messages.
fields.chunkIdsHint: Up to 2 book + 2 country chunks are selected per request; order matters for priority.

knowledge.live: Live in database — edge functions load published chunks
knowledge.draftOnly: Draft / not live — app uses bundled fallback until you publish

settings.title: Settings
settings.deleteAccountConfirmMessage: This permanently deletes your Beaverr account, cloud data, and everything stored on this device. You cannot undo this.
settings.deleteAccountFailed: Could not delete your account. Please try again.
settings.deleteAccountNotConfigured: Account deletion is not enabled on the server yet. Run the delete-account migration or deploy the delete-account Edge Function.
